query: iPhone complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Apple is rated "Poor" with 1.8 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Evaluating 1,519 reviews, most reviewers were unhappy with their experience overall. Many customers expressed dissatisfaction with the customer service, finding it unhelpful and difficult to get issues resolved, often experiencing long wait times and uncooperative staff. Reviewers also encountered significant problems with the products themselves, including devices not working as expected, issues with compatibility, and concerns about durability and repair costs. The user experience with the app and overall ecosystem was frequently criticized for being unintuitive, difficult to navigate, and having frustrating account activation processes. Additionally, many people felt that the pricing of products and repairs was excessively high, especially given the perceived decline in quality and [...] ### Was this summary helpful?

### Reviews shaping this summary

However, some customers also noted positive aspects, such as excellent noise cancellation in newer products and satisfaction with certain devices fitting well within the ecosystem. A few other people also felt that the customer service was professional and helpful on multiple occasions, and some found the products to be generally excellent and functional.See more

results.title: Apple Service and Repair for iPhone
results.content:
Learn more

Thanks for your feedback. [...] Our warranty doesn’t cover accidental damage or batteries that wear down from normal use. If your situation isn’t covered and consumer law doesn’t apply, there will be a cost associated with repairs through Apple. If your issue is ineligible for service, you may need to pay the full value for a replacement device. Check your coverage status here.

#### Is there a service guarantee? [...] #### Where can I find information about my repairs?

You can sign in to My Support or the Apple Support app with your Apple Account to get information about current or recent repairs.

#### What’s covered under warranty?

The Apple Limited Warranty covers your device and all the Apple-branded accessories that come in the box with your product against manufacturing issues for one year from the date you bought them. Apple-branded accessories that are purchased separately, like adapters, cables, wireless chargers, or cases, are covered by the Apple Limited Warranty for Accessories. Our warranty is in addition to rights provided by consumer law in your country or region.

results.title: Find information about your AppleCare plan - Apple Support
results.content:
# Find information about your AppleCare plan

Check if your device is covered by an AppleCare plan and find details about your AppleCare coverage.

## Check if your device is covered by an AppleCare plan

To find AppleCare coverage information, you might be able to open AppleCare & Warranty settings on your device. Or, open Settings, select General, then select AppleCare & Warranty. You can also find your plan when you check your coverage online.

Learn what to do if you can't find your AppleCare plan

Not all AppleCare products are available in all countries or regions.

## Find your AppleCare coverage expiration date [...] ## Get the agreement number or proof of coverage

1. Go to mysupport.apple.com.
2. Sign in to your Apple Account.
3. Choose your device.
4. Click View Proof of Coverage. If you don't see your proof of coverage, make sure that you set up your Apple Account with two-factor authentication.

## Need more help?

Tell us more about what's happening, and we’ll suggest what you can do next.

Get suggestions

Published Date:

Thanks for your feedback.

### Related topics [...] ## Find your AppleCare coverage expiration date

 On your iPhone or iPad, go to Settings, then tap General. Tap AppleCare & Warranty, then tap your device.
 On your Mac, click the Apple menu in the corner of your screen. Choose System Settings, then click General. Click AppleCare & Warranty, then select your device.
 Go to mysupport.apple.com and sign in to your Apple Account. Then choose your device to see details about the support that you're eligible for — including hardware repairs and technical support.

## Get the agreement number or proof of coverage
